portfolio_id.description: Primary Key for database. Use UUID or unique slug.

user_id.description: Foreign Key for database linking to user. Use UUID or unique slug.

portfolio_name.Label: Portfolio Name
portfolio_name.description: Name of the portfolio for example - My Portfolio, Retirement Portfolio, etc.

portfolio_current_corpus.Label: Current Corpus of the Portfolio
portfolio_current_corpus.description: Current total corpus of the portfolio based on the corpus added by the user for each instrument. This should be system calculated based on the sum of ytd corpus for all instruments in the portfolio.

portfolio_current_equity_allocation.Label: Current Equity Allocation for the Portfolio
portfolio_current_equity_allocation.description: Current percentage of equity allocation for the portfolio based on the corpus added by the user for each instrument and their asset type. This should be system calculated based on the sum of ytd corpus for all equity instruments in the portfolio divided by the total portfolio corpus.

portfolio_current_debt_allocation.Label: Current Debt Allocation for the Portfolio
portfolio_current_debt_allocation.description: Current percentage of debt allocation for the portfolio based on the corpus added by the user for each instrument and their asset type. This should be system calculated based on the sum of ytd corpus for all debt instruments in the portfolio divided by the total portfolio corpus.

portfolio_current_gold_allocation.Label: Current Gold Allocation for the Portfolio
portfolio_current_gold_allocation.description: Current percentage of gold allocation for the portfolio based on the corpus added by the user for each instrument and their asset type. This should be system calculated based on the sum of ytd corpus for all gold instruments in the portfolio divided by the total portfolio corpus.

portfolio_description.Label: Describe your Portfolio
portfolio_description.description: Description of the portfolio which can include details about the portfolio strategy, risk profile, etc.

goal_id.description: Primary Key for database. Use UUID or unique slug.

goal_name.Label: Goal Name
goal_name.description: Name of the financial goal for example - Retirement, Child's Education, etc.

target_year.Label: Which year to Achieve the Goal?
target_year.description: Target year for the financial goal which will be used to calculate the time horizon for investments

target_corpus.Label: Corpus Required at Target Year?
target_corpus.description: Target corpus for the financial goal at the target year

goal_current_corpus.Label: Current Corpus for the Goal
goal_current_corpus.description: Current corpus for the financial goal based on the corpus added by the user for each instrument linked to the goal. This should be system calculated based on the sum of ytd corpus for all instruments linked to the goal.

goal_progress_percentage.Label: Progress Percentage towards the Goal
goal_progress_percentage.description: Progress percentage towards the financial goal based on the current corpus and the target corpus. This should be system calculated based on the formula - Progress Percentage = (Current Corpus / Target Corpus) * 100

goal_expected_equity_allocation.Label: Expected Equity Allocation for the Goal?
goal_expected_equity_allocation.description: Expected percentage of equity allocation for the financial goal

goal_expected_debt_allocation.Label: Expected Debt Allocation for the Goal?
goal_expected_debt_allocation.description: Expected percentage of debt allocation for the financial goal

goal_expected_gold_allocation.Label: Expected Gold Allocation for the Goal?
goal_expected_gold_allocation.description: Expected percentage of gold allocation for the financial goal

goal_current_equity_allocation.Label: Current Equity Allocation for the Goal?
goal_current_equity_allocation.description: Current percentage of equity allocation for the financial goal based on the corpus added by the user

goal_current_debt_allocation.Label: Current Debt Allocation for the Goal?
goal_current_debt_allocation.description: Current percentage of debt allocation for the financial goal based on the corpus added by the user

goal_current_gold_allocation.Label: Current Gold Allocation for the Goal?
goal_current_gold_allocation.description: Current percentage of gold allocation for the financial goal based on the corpus added by the user

instrument_id.description: Primary Key for database. Use UUID or unique slug.

instrument_name.Label: Instrument Name
instrument_name.description: Name of the financial instrument for example - HDFC Nifty 50 Index Fund

instrument_type.Label: Instrument Type
instrument_type.description: Type of the financial instrument for example - Mutual Fund

instrument_status.Label: Instrument Status
instrument_status.description: Status of the financial instrument for example - Active, Paused, Stopped. This can be used to exclude certain investments from calculations without deleting them

fund_category.Label: Fund Category
fund_category.description: Visible only for mutual funds instrument type, can be used for categorization, benchmarking and filtering

asset_type.Label: Asset Category
asset_type.description: Asset type of the financial instrument for example - Equity, Debt, Hybrid, Gold. Incase of Hybrid, should ask the Equity/Debt percentage split. This can be used for asset allocation, benchmarking and filtering

asset_sub_type.Label: Asset Sub-Category
asset_sub_type.description: Asset sub-type of the financial instrument for example - Equity India, Equity US, Debt Government, Debt Corporate, Hybrid, Gold etc. This can be used for asset allocation, benchmarking and filtering

frequency.Label: Frequency
frequency.description: Frequency of the financial instrument for example - Monthly, Annually. This can be used for future forecasting, asset allocation, benchmarking and filtering.

start_date.Label: Start Date
start_date.description: Start date of the investment in the financial instrument.

end_date.Label: End Date
end_date.description: Possible End date of the investment in the financial instrument.

expected_roi.Label: Expected ROI
expected_roi.description: Expected rate of return for the financial instrument which can be used for forecasting. This can be a user input or system calculated based on historical returns or benchmarks. For example, for mutual funds we can calculate this based on 3/5/10 year CAGR or based on category benchmark returns.

expected_corpus.Label: Projected Wealth
expected_corpus.description: Expected corpus from the financial instrument at the end date based on the expected ROI and the schedule of investments. This should be system calculated based on the formula - FV(roi, time, payments, pv)

ytd_corpus.Label: Current Wealth YTD
ytd_corpus.description: User enters current corpus for manual tracking of the instrument.

step_up_type.Label: Step-Up Type
step_up_type.description: Type of step-up for the financial instrument for example - SalaryLinked, Manual, AutomaticYoY and Not Applicable.

step_up_percentage.Label: Step-Up Percentage for Automatic YoY
step_up_percentage.description: Percentage increase for each step-up for Automatic YoY step-up type only. The system will calculate the amount based on the previous step-up amount and this percentage increase.

instrument_start_amount.Label: Initial Investment Amount
instrument_start_amount.description: Initial investment amount for the financial instrument which will be used as the base amount for calculating step-ups.

schedule.description: Schedule of step-ups for the financial instrument for these step up types: For Not Applicable, the step-up will not be applicable. For Manual, user will input the amount and the date for each step-up and can change it later. For AutomaticYoY, user will input the percentage increase for each step-up and the system will calculate the amount based on the previous step-up amount. For SalaryLinked, the step-up will be linked to the user's salary growth which can be calculated based on probable future salary growth and will be calculated automatically.

schedule_id.description: Primary Key for database. Use UUID or unique slug.

schedule_name.Label: Schedule Name
schedule_name.description: Name of the step-up schedule for example - Initial Investment, First Step-Up, etc.

date.Label: Step-Up Date
date.description: Date of the step-up for the financial instrument. Only visible for Manual step-up types.

amount.Label: Step-Up Amount
amount.description: Amount of the step-up for the financial instrument. Only visible for Manual step-up types.

goal_tax_liability.Label: Estimated Tax Liability on Goal on End Date
goal_tax_liability.description: Estimated tax liability for the financial goal at the target year based on the expected corpus and the applicable tax rates. This should be system calculated based on the formula - Tax = (STCG * stcg_percentage) + (LTCG * ltcg_percentage) + (Cess * cess_percentage)

instrument_tax_liability.Label: Estimated Tax Liability on Instrument on End Date
instrument_tax_liability.description: Estimated tax liability for the financial instrument at the end date based on the expected corpus and the applicable tax rates. This should be system calculated based on the formula - Tax = (STCG * stcg_percentage) + (LTCG * ltcg_percentage) + (Cess * cess_percentage)

instrument_tax_liability_now.Label: Current Tax Liability if Redeemed Today on Instrument
instrument_tax_liability_now.description: Estimated tax liability for the financial instrument today based on the ytd corpus and the investment amount on the instrument following applicable tax rates. This should be system calculated based on the formula - Tax = (STCG * stcg_percentage) + (LTCG * ltcg_percentage) + (Cess * cess_percentage)

hybrid_allocation.description: Allocation details for hybrid instruments to calculate the asset allocation, expected returns and tax liability accurately. This should include the percentage allocation to equity, debt and gold for hybrid instruments.

hybrid_allocation_id.description: Primary Key for database. Use UUID or unique slug.

allocation_type.Label: Allocation Type
allocation_type.description: Type of allocation for hybrid instruments for example - Equity India, Debt Government, etc. This can be used for asset allocation, benchmarking and filtering.

allocation_percentage.Label: Allocation Percentage
allocation_percentage.description: Percentage allocation for the allocation type in hybrid instruments. The sum of all allocation percentages for a hybrid instrument should be 100%.

transaction_id.description: Primary Key for database. Use UUID or unique slug.

transaction_date.Label: Transaction Date
transaction_date.description: Date of the transaction for the financial instrument.

transaction_amount.Label: Transaction Amount
transaction_amount.description: Amount of the transaction for the financial instrument.

stamp_duty.Label: Stamp Duty on Transaction
stamp_duty.description: Stamp duty for the transaction based on the applicable rates. This should be system calculated based on the formula - Stamp Duty = transaction_amount * stamp_duty_rate

nav.Label: NAV on Transaction Date
nav.description: NAV of the financial instrument on the transaction date. This can be used to calculate the units purchased for mutual funds and ETFs.

units_purchased.Label: Units Purchased
units_purchased.description: Units purchased for the transaction calculated based on the transaction amount, stamp duty and NAV for mutual funds and ETFs. This should be system calculated based on the formula - Units Purchased = (transaction_amount - stamp_duty) / nav

unit_balance.Label: Unit Balance after Transaction
unit_balance.description: Unit balance for the financial instrument after the transaction. This should be system calculated based on the previous unit balance and the units purchased or sold in the transaction.

transaction_type.Label: Transaction Type
transaction_type.description: Type of the transaction for the financial instrument for example - BUY, SELL, DIVIDEND. This can be used for calculating the current corpus, expected corpus, tax liability and for filtering transactions.

portfolio_data.portfolio_description: This is where the actual data would sit, following the rules above.
